newsSince: No new fundamental catalyst since June 9 beyond price action. Q1 (record NT$38.2B revenue, net income +45%, AI mix ~15%, repeated MLCC/tantalum price hikes) was reported in April and is fully consensus. The stock ran another ~23% in two weeks to +323% YTD, making it one of the largest TWSE names - intensifying crowding without fresh bottleneck evidence.

sources.label: BigGo - Yageo AI revenue hits 15%, chairman hosts summit

verdicts.rankRationale: Crowding-sensitive residual-alpha lens: no new fundamentals since June 9, yet another +23% run to +323% YTD makes it a mega-cap consensus name. The MLCC/pricing-power story is real but fully owned; residual alpha keeps compressing from the already-crowded benchmark slot. Thesis-driven down.
verdicts.bull: Record Q1, real MLCC/tantalum pricing power, AI mix to exceed 15%; high-end passive undersupply persists.
verdicts.neutral: Genuine pricing power, but it is now consensus and the price has captured it.
verdicts.bear: +323% YTD, top-10 TWSE name, no fresh catalyst - textbook over-owned, low-residual-alpha setup.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued MLCC/tantalum price hikes into AI servers

verdicts.rankRationale: Board-level power/passives lens: the record Q1 (+47%/+45%) and confirmed pricing power are the execution evidence the thesis asked to watch, but nothing new arrived since June 9 - it is the same April print. On this broader lens the validated power-delivery content keeps rank steady; no incremental shift.
verdicts.bull: Power-delivery content (MLCC/inductors) scaling with AI server PDN; demonstrated pricing power and record backlog.
verdicts.neutral: Strong execution already validated and priced; growth rate likely moderates into Q2.
verdicts.bear: Cyclical passives at peak multiples; little upside left after the rerating.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI-server PDN content gains and price increases
